emergent_themes.description: How dramatic changes in economic context (moving countries, student vs working life) fundamentally alter delivery behavior patterns
Using group orders as a primary strategy to make delivery financially viable for cost-sensitive users
Latent demand from users with positive past subscription experiences who would reactivate under different economic circumstances
How international students' price sensitivity is amplified by currency conversion considerations

coding_summary: Apollo's interview reveals a participant whose delivery behavior has been fundamentally shaped by economic constraints following his transition from working life in Kenya to student life in Dublin. His extremely disciplined approach to food (eating the same meals daily for fitness goals) contrasts with his sophisticated understanding of delivery platforms and subscription value propositions. Key insights include his exclusive reliance on group ordering to split costs, his detailed platform comparison behavior, and his clear intention to resume heavy delivery usage when returning to Kenya. The interview provides strong evidence for how economic context shifts can completely alter delivery behavior patterns, while maintaining underlying engagement and platform knowledge.

coverage_gaps.reason: Apollo's highly regimented eating routine means he doesn't have distinct eating occasions - he eats the same foods daily regardless of context
